title: Withdrawals, transfers, and reentrancy considerations
scope: File: stack_wbnb.sol (withdraw, transfer, sell), proxy_wbnb.sol (buyFor transfer to user)
what_to_reason: Reentrancy possibilities around external token transfers
Checks-effects-interactions ordering, especially in `withdraw` which calls token.transfer
The safety of calling withdraw() within transfer() when myDividends()>0
Whether `token.transfer` could trigger arbitrary code or revert and cause state inconsistencies
how_to_reason: Identify external calls that transfer tokens and ensure state is updated before calls
Enumerate reentrancy scenarios if token is malicious or an ERC777-style token
Assess if `onlyBagholders` / `onlyStronghands` modifiers properly gate functions
Consider use of reentrancy guard or pull-over-push patterns for safety
edge_cases: Token contract that calls back into Stack during `token.transfer`
Partial transfer failures and the implications for payoutsTo_ and stats
Transfers to contracts with fallback that consume gas or revert
